title: EvidenceExtremes: Extreme Value Theory Characterises the Lower Tail of Cochrane Meta-Analysis Trust Scores
summary: Extreme value analysis of 6,229 Cochrane meta-analyses reveals a near-Gumbel GPD lower tail (xi=0.039) with domain-specific heavy-tail risk concentrated in heterogeneous clinical categories.
body: Among 6,229 Cochrane meta-analyses, 271 (4.4%) score below 50, defining the F-grade extreme-quality tail. Generalised Pareto distribution fitting to exceedances below this threshold yields shape xi=0.039, classifying the lower tail as near-Gumbel with mildly exponential decay. Generalised extreme value fitting to domain-level block minima produces shape xi=4.32, confirming a heavy Frechet regime across 14 clinical domains. The Other domain carries the heaviest GPD tail index (xi=0.061), followed by Hypertension (xi=0.034), while Cancer and Gastrointestinal show bounded Weibull tails (xi<-1). Empirical 1-in-50, 1-in-100, and 1-in-500 return levels correspond to trust scores of 44, 43, and 41, indicating a hard floor near the minimum observed score of 19. QQ concordance between empirical and GEV quantiles confirms distributional model adequacy. These findings quantify systematic lower-tail risk in Cochrane evidence and enable domain-specific extreme-quality surveillance.

checks.name: QQ correlation > 0.99
checks.detail: Pearson r on generated GEV data

included_papers.title: EvidenceScore: Trust-score pipeline for Cochrane MAs
included_papers.citation: Internal pipeline. 6,229 MAs scored across 5 domains.

analyses.label: GPD overall
analyses.summary: GPD fit on 271 exceedances below threshold 50. xi=0.039 (near-Gumbel), scale=2.98.

analyses.label: GEV block minima
analyses.summary: GEV fit on 14 domain block minima. xi=4.32 (Frechet), loc=20.27, scale=5.49.
analyses.details: Negation trick: fit on -block_minima, loc back-transformed to mu_min=-loc.

analyses.label: Tail by domain
analyses.summary: GPD per domain. Other (xi=0.061) > Hypertension (xi=0.034) > Diabetes (xi=-0.040).
analyses.details: Dermatology, Neurological, Renal: no exceedances (all scores >= 50).

analyses.label: Return levels
analyses.details: GEV-based return levels are astronomically negative due to large xi=4.32 with n=14.
